chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِرَادَةِ أَنَّهَا مِنْ صِفَاتِ الْفِعْلِ وَ سَائِرِ صِفَاتِ الْفِعْلِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – The Intention (Will), it is from the Attributes of the deed, and the rest of the Attributes of the deed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/16)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/16)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) أَخْبِرْنِي عَنِ الْإِرَادَةِ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ مِنَ الْخَلْقِ قَالَ فَقَالَ الْإِرَادَةُ مِنَ الْخَلْقِ الضَّمِيرُ وَ مَا يَبْدُو لَهُمْ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْفِعْلِ وَ أَمَّا مِنَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى فَإِرَادَتُهُ إِحْدَاثُهُ لَا غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهُ لَا يُرَوِّي وَ لَا يَهُمُّ وَ لَا يَتَفَكَّرُ وَ هَذِهِ الصِّفَاتُ مَنْفِيَّةٌ عَنْهُ وَ هِيَ صِفَاتُ الْخَلْقِ
فَإِرَادَةُ اللَّهِ الْفِعْلُ لَا غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ بِلَا لَفْظٍ وَ لَا نُطْقٍ بِلِسَانٍ وَ لَا هِمَّةٍ وَ لَا تَفَكُّرٍ وَ لَا كَيْفَ لِذَلِكَ كَمَا أَنَّهُ لَا كَيْفَ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan Bin Yahya who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Inform me about the Intention (Will) from Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and from the creature’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Intention from the creature (people) is the conscience and begins for them after that from the deed, and as for (the Intention) from Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Exalted, so His<sup>azwj</sup> Intention is His<sup>azwj</sup> Creation and not other than that, because He<sup>azwj</sup> neither narrates, nor whims, nor thinks, and these Attributes are negated from Him<sup>azwj</sup>, and these are the attributes of the creatures.

So the Intention of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is the deed, not other than that. He<sup>azwj</sup> is Saying to it, ‘Be’, so it occurs (comes into being), without a letter spoken by a tongue, nor a whim, nor thinking, nor is there a ‘where’ for that just as there is no ‘where’ (qualitative state) for Him<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Idris has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan ibn Yahya who has said the following. “I asked abu al-Hassan (a.s.), ‘Enlighten me about the will of Allah and the will of His creatures.’” He said, “The will of His creatures comes from what goes in their minds and leads to action. Will of Allah, the Most High, is His inventing and nothing else because Allah does not need to reflect, deliberate or think. Such qualities do not exist with Him. They are the attributes of His creation. Allah’s will is His acts and nothing else. He says to it: Be, and it comes into existence, without any words or utterance of the tongue, any inclination and reflection. His will has no conditions just as His self has no conditions.”
